Division 2 Missions Manhunts Lore About YouTube

The Division Universe / Evidence-led archive

The Division Lore Archive

A source-led archive of The Division lore, characters, events, collectibles and documented real-world influences.

No source, no claimCanon and theory stay separateEvery page is versioned
47structured records47source reviewed
CoverageVerified counts and acknowledged gaps TimelineSourced sequence without invented dates MethodologyEvidence, canon and review rules Review queueRecords awaiting human approval CopyrightTranscript and quotation boundaries Evidence intakeSubmit collectible and audio captures CorrectionsReport an error with evidence SourcesShared provenance registry

1 record

Diseases

Structured, source-linked records in this category.

disease

Green Poison

The engineered smallpox outbreak also called the Dollar Flu, whose Black Friday spread drives the collapse depicted across The Division franchise.

not applicablesource reviewed

5 records

Events

Structured, source-linked records in this category.

event

Black Tusk Siege of Washington

The endgame invasion in which Black Tusk enters Washington, places the city under siege and reveals its campaign against SHD.

not applicablesource reviewed

event

Division Network Crisis

The nationwide SHD Network failure and Washington distress call that draw Division Agents to the capital at the beginning of The Division 2.

not applicablesource reviewed

event

Green Poison Outbreak

The Black Friday biological outbreak that breaks public order, overwhelms response systems and begins the central crisis of The Division universe.

not applicablesource reviewed

event

Lower Manhattan Biological Attack

The attack that kills civilians and SHD Agents in Lower Manhattan and triggers the Warlords of New York hunt for Aaron Keener.

not applicablesource reviewed

event

SHD Activation

The emergency activation of secretly trained Division Agents after the Green Poison outbreak overwhelms ordinary response systems.

not applicablesource reviewed

8 records

Factions

Structured, source-linked records in this category.

faction

Black Tusk

An advanced private military faction that sabotages the Division Network, invades Washington and pursues national control through high-end weapons and robotics.

not applicablesource reviewed

faction

Cleaners

A faction formed from sanitation workers and first responders who attempt to eradicate infection through fire.

not applicablesource reviewed

faction

Hyenas

A hostile Washington faction recognizable by gas masks and, in later seasonal material, an expanding Spice operation tied to the Dark Zones.

not applicablesource reviewed

faction

Last Man Battalion

A hostile Manhattan faction that cooperates with Aaron Keener and retains a command structure after the first game's events.

not applicablesource reviewed

faction

Outcasts

A hostile Division 2 faction whose continued activity and developing alliance with the True Sons and Hyenas are confirmed in official seasonal material.

not applicablesource reviewed

faction

Peacekeepers

A friendly Lower Manhattan survivor group formed by civilians and former Joint Task Force members.

not applicablesource reviewed

faction

Rikers

A faction originating with escaped Rikers Island prisoners and later recruits, reorganized into splinter groups after the first game.

not applicablesource reviewed

faction

True Sons

An organized paramilitary faction using former military and JTF expertise, heavy firepower and territorial control in Washington.

not applicablesource reviewed

10 records

Locations

Structured, source-linked records in this category.

location

Camp White Oak

A remote woodland estate used as a strategic Black Tusk position and extraction site for President Ellis.

not applicablesource reviewed

location

Capitol Hill

The True Sons strongpoint where President Ellis and the encryption key needed to reach the broad-spectrum antiviral are held.

not applicablesource reviewed

location

Coney Island

The New York setting of Episode 3, divided between a Black Tusk operation at the ballpark and a Cleaner presence at the amusement park.

not applicablesource reviewed

location

Lower Manhattan

The New York setting of Warlords of New York, divided among returning factions and rogue Agents during the hunt for Aaron Keener.

not applicablesource reviewed

location

Manhattan Dark Zone

The initial containment zone where first-wave Agents are abandoned and where supply drops create a dangerous scavenging economy.

not applicablesource reviewed

location

Manning National Zoo

The Episode 1 location fortified by the Outcasts after Emeline Shaw retreats from Roosevelt Island.

not applicablesource reviewed

location

Midtown Manhattan

The central operating area Division Agents are activated to secure against the Rikers, Cleaners and Last Man Battalion.

not applicablesource reviewed

location

The Pentagon

The abandoned and storm-damaged military headquarters placed under siege by Black Tusk during Episode 2.

not applicablesource reviewed

location

Tidal Basin

Black Tusk's principal Washington stronghold and the site where Division Agents recover the broad-spectrum antivirals.

not applicablesource reviewed

location

Washington, D.C.

The primary setting of The Division 2, where institutional collapse, civilian reconstruction and competing factions converge around the U.S. capital.

not applicablesource reviewed

7 records

Missions

Structured, source-linked records in this category.

mission

Camp White Oak

An Episode 1 mission in which Division Agents pursue President Ellis at a Black Tusk position outside Washington.

not applicablesource reviewed

mission

Coney Island Amusement Park

An Episode 3 main mission centered on a Cleaner presence at Coney Island's amusement park.

not applicablesource reviewed

mission

Coney Island Ballpark

An Episode 3 main mission to rescue virologist Vitaly Tchernenko from Black Tusk captivity at Coney Island.

not applicablesource reviewed

mission

DARPA Research Labs

The second Episode 2 main mission, focused on stopping Black Tusk from extracting strategically valuable DARPA prototypes beneath the Pentagon.

not applicablesource reviewed

mission

Manning National Zoo

An Episode 1 mission that sends Division Agents through Outcast defenses to confront faction leader Emeline Shaw.

not applicablesource reviewed

mission

The Pentagon

The first Episode 2 main mission inside the besieged Pentagon, where Agents investigate Black Tusk's search for strategic resources.

not applicablesource reviewed

mission

Tidal Basin

The operation against Black Tusk's principal Washington stronghold that ends with Division Agents recovering the stolen broad-spectrum antivirals.

not applicablesource reviewed

2 records

Organizations

Structured, source-linked records in this category.

organization

Joint Task Force

The first-responder organization commonly called the JTF, which attempts to contain the outbreak before SHD activation and later contributes veterans to survivor groups.

not applicablesource reviewed

organization

Strategic Homeland Division

The secret emergency organization commonly called the Division or SHD, whose trained civilian Agents are activated when national systems fail.

not applicablesource reviewed

12 records

People

Structured, source-linked records in this category.

person

Aaron Keener

A first-wave Division Agent who went rogue and became the central target of the Warlords of New York manhunt.

not applicablesource reviewed

person

Andrew Ellis

The former Speaker who becomes President during the crisis, is rescued from the True Sons and is later exposed as a Black Tusk collaborator.

not applicablesource reviewed

person

Emeline Shaw

The Outcasts' leader, pursued from Roosevelt Island to a final confrontation at Manning National Zoo.

not applicablesource reviewed

person

Faye Lau

A Division Agent whose appearance in the early Division 2 recap demonstrates the restored SHD Network reconnecting Agents across the country.

not applicablesource reviewed

person

Gordon Amherst

The virologist identified by the Division as the creator of Green Poison.

not applicablesource reviewed

person

James Dragov

A rogue lieutenant of Aaron Keener who leads the Rikers sub-group operating in Lower Manhattan.

not applicablesource reviewed

person

Jessica Kandel

A doctor and surviving scientist involved in the nationwide effort to create a broad-spectrum antiviral.

not applicablesource reviewed

person

Joe Ferro

The original leader of the Cleaners, who made fire the faction's method for eliminating Green Poison.

not applicablesource reviewed

person

Larae Barrett

The former Rikers leader whose death leaves the faction leaderless and divided into multiple sub-groups.

not applicablesource reviewed

person

Rick Valassi

A reporter and broadcaster who remains on the air after the events of the first game.

not applicablesource reviewed

person

Vitaly Tchernenko

A Russian virologist pursued by multiple factions because his expertise could support mass production of a broad-spectrum antiviral.

not applicablesource reviewed

person

Vivian Conley

The Cleaner leader in Warlords of New York and one of Aaron Keener's four lieutenants.

not applicablesource reviewed

2 records

Real-world influences

Structured, source-linked records in this category.

government directive

Directive 51 (NSPD-51 / HSPD-20)

A real 2007 United States continuity directive that The Division uses as the fictional legal foundation for the Strategic Homeland Division.

confirmed influencesource reviewed

real world event

Operation Dark Winter

A 2001 senior-level tabletop exercise built around a fictional smallpox attack, and a documented real-world influence on the premise of The Division.

confirmed influencesource reviewed

Unofficial, source-led research into The Division universe and its real-world influences.

Lore Archive / Methodology / Corrections / YouTube @raigulus
